rationale: The signal set is dominated by broad-intent searches (enclosure, care, price, diet) and inspirational/art content from Bluesky. Very few forums or breeding-specific discussions surfaced. However, three high-demand breeding/genetics angles emerged: (1) YouTube repeatedly surfaces warnings about incompatible pairings and warnings about Super Cappuccinos, (2) "morph calculator" is a top autocomplete, suggesting confusion between prediction tools and field reality, and (3) Lilly White hatchling identification is a recurrent query, with viewers asking how to ID juvenile color transitions. Each of these solves a real breeder pain point, has minimal coverage in our published archive, and can be grounded in genetics basics (polygenic modifiers, incomplete dominance color-shifting, behavioral selection). Rejected pure care topics (enclosure, diet, general breeding season) as already covered or too general. Rejected morph-specific listicles without a through-line. These three clear the bar on originality (fresh angles, not rehashes) and voice fit (breeder-to-breeder, actionable, myth-busting tone).
